problem_solved: How can a learning card be trimmed without losing the information needed for retrieval, routing, and upgrade?
core_idea: Rank sections by answer-criticality, routing value, and uniqueness, then cut the lowest-yield material first.
method_structure: State the dominant question of the card.
Keep the sections that answer or bound that question.
Keep graph sections only when they carry real local value.
Gate progression scaffolding by maturity or review mode.
Render the shortest version that still supports reuse.
design_choices: The method prefers signal density over template completeness.
hidden_assumptions: The reviewer can tell the difference between useful nuance and habitual filler.
fit_scenarios: Use it when cards feel complete but still hard to scan or retrieve from.
non_fit_scenarios: Do not use it when the card is weak because the topic itself is still unclear.
comparison_with_alternatives: Unlike a purely aesthetic rewrite, this method cuts by information yield rather than by sentence style alone.
common_misuses: Removing graph sections too early and leaving the card isolated.
Keeping every checklist because it feels productive.
failure_modes: The method fails when the reviewer trims high-value boundary sections together with low-value scaffolding.
decision_criteria: If a section neither changes retrieval, graph placement, nor next action, it is a compression candidate.
representative_examples: Merging status notes, next goal, and upgrade tasks into one short maintenance block while preserving the concept core and local graph placement.
validation_question: After trimming, can a future self still answer what this card is for, where it fits, and what to do next?

upstream_concepts: [[Single-Card Boundary]] | Clear boundaries make section ranking easier.
upstream_mechanisms: [[Section Bloat Drift]] | The method is a response to that drift.
adjacent_methods: [[Template Rewrite]] | Both reduce verbosity, but this method uses information yield as the ranking rule.

prerequisites: [[Card Type Routing]] | Section compression assumes the right card type is already chosen.
enables: [[Default Stable Render]] | It produces a thinner default card shape.
contrasts: [[Complete Template Filling]] | One rewards completeness; the other rewards signal density.
corrections: [[More Sections Make a Better Learning Card]] | The method corrects that impulse operationally.

direct_routes: When the problem is conceptual rather than operational, return to [[Single-Card Boundary]].
When the problem is repeated re-expansion after trimming, route to [[More Sections Make a Better Learning Card]].
secondary_routes: If [[Section Bloat Drift]] explains the cause but you still need an execution order, return here and trim by ranking.
gap_signals: If the graph can explain what to cut but not how to measure retrieval quality after trimming, add a new Concept card for retrieval density.
stop_rules: Stop trimming when removing one more section would erase boundary clarity, graph placement, or next action.

current_status_notes: The method already works as a reliable local procedure for slimming stable cards.
next_goal: Add a lightweight scoring shortcut for faster review passes.
stable_checklist: Show at least one repeated successful trim pattern.
expert_ready_checklist: Add stronger arbitration between compression, rerouting, and splitting.

main_reasons: The method already has stable local execution value.
missing_evidence: It still needs stronger arbitration between trimming a card and splitting it into two cards.
next_rules: If compression removes a whole second question, split the card instead of trimming harder.

current_upgrade_tasks: Capture one borderline case where splitting beat trimming.
